reason: The statement does not apply to the text fragment provided. In this narrative, Jacob is portrayed as a central character who is interacting with God and his brother Esau. While Jacob may have faced challenges and struggles, there is no indication that Yahweh sees all the people as evil with the exception of one righteous person. Jacob himself is portrayed as seeking blessings and favor from God, and his encounter with Esau ends in reconciliation rather than judgment.
